---
title: "Statistical Test: Key Code"
subtitle: "Applied Statistics"
affiliation: "Kyiv School of Economics"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import binom
from scipy.stats import binomtest
from statsmodels.stats.proportion import proportion_confint

np.random.seed(73)

turquoise = "#20B2AA"
red       = "#fb6107"
blue      = "#181485"
```

## Binomial distribution

The test statistic $Q$ counts the number of successes in $n$ independent Bernoulli trials:

$$Q \sim \text{Binomial}(n, p)$$

**Probability mass function** $p_\xi(x) = P(\xi = x)$:

```{python}
n = 30   # number of trials
p = 0.5  # probability of success under H0

# P(Q = 10), P(Q = 15), etc.
binom.pmf(10, n, p).item()
```

```{python}
binom.pmf(15, n, p).item()
```

```{python}
# Probabilities for all values 1..30
x_grid = np.arange(1, n + 1)
probs   = binom.pmf(x_grid, n, p)
```

---

## Critical region

We reject $H_0$ when $Q \geq C$. The critical value $C$ is chosen so that
$\text{FPR} = P(Q \geq C \mid H_0) \leq \alpha$.

```{python}
# FPR for C = 20
crit_reg = x_grid >= 20
probs[crit_reg].sum().item()
```

```{python}
# FPR for C = 19  (too large — exceeds 10%)
crit_reg_19 = x_grid >= 19
probs[crit_reg_19].sum().item()
```

**Cumulative distribution function** $F_\xi(x) = P(\xi \leq x)$:

```{python}
binom.cdf(19, 30, 0.5).item()
```

```{python}
# P(Q >= 20) = 1 - P(Q <= 19)
1 - binom.cdf(19, 30, 0.5).item()
```

---

## Quantile

The $p$-quantile is the smallest $x$ such that $F_\xi(x) \geq p$.

```{python}
binom.cdf(18, 30, 0.5).item()
```

```{python}
binom.cdf(19, 30, 0.5).item()
```

```{python}
binom.cdf(20, 30, 0.5).item()
```

```{python}
# Direct computation: binom.ppf(p, n, p_success)
int(binom.ppf(0.95, 30, 0.5))
```

---

## Custom test function

For any $n$, $\mu_0$, and $\alpha$ the critical value is $C = u_{1-\alpha} + 1$:

```{python}
def make_binom_test(n, mu=0.5, alpha=0.05):
    q = int(binom.ppf(1 - alpha, n, mu))
    return q + 1

print(f"If Q >= {make_binom_test(30, 0.5, 0.05)} → reject H0")
```

Additional example ($n = 50$, $\mu_0 = 0.1$):

```{python}
np.random.seed(73)

n = 50
p = 0.1

binom.rvs(n, p, size=3)  # generate random observations
```

```{python}
print(f"If Q >= {make_binom_test(n=50, mu=0.1, alpha=0.05)} → reject H0")
```

---

## $p$-value

The $p$-value is the probability of observing a result at least as extreme as $q$, assuming $H_0$ is true.

$$p\text{-value} = P(Q \geq q \mid H_0)$$

```{python}
def pvalue_binom(n, mu, q):
    return 1 - binom.cdf(q - 1, n, mu)
```

```{python}
# Delivery task: n=30, q=19, H0: mu=0.5
p_val = pvalue_binom(30, 0.5, 19)
suffix = "<= 0.05" if p_val <= 0.05 else ">= 0.05"
print(f"p-value = {round(p_val, 3)} {suffix}")
```

```{python}
# Additional example: n=50, q=11, H0: mu=0.1
p_val = pvalue_binom(50, 0.1, 11)
suffix = "<= 0.05" if p_val <= 0.05 else ">= 0.05"
print(f"p-value = {round(p_val, 3)} {suffix}")
```

### Built-in: `scipy.stats.binomtest`

```{python}
# n=30, q=19, H1: mu > 0.5
result = binomtest(k=19, n=30, p=0.5, alternative="greater")
print(f"p-value: {result.pvalue:.4f}")
print(f"95% CI:  {result.proportion_ci(confidence_level=0.95)}")
```

```{python}
# n=50, q=11, H1: mu > 0.1
result2 = binomtest(k=11, n=50, p=0.1, alternative="greater")
print(f"p-value: {result2.pvalue:.4f}")
```

---

## Two-sided test

When $H_1: \mu \neq \mu_0$ the rejection region is symmetric (or split into two tails).

### Symmetric distribution ($\mu_0 = 0.5$)

```{python}
def pvalue_two_sided_sum(n, q):
    binom_h0 = binom.pmf(np.arange(1, n + 1), n, 0.5)
    diff = abs(q - 15)
    right_sq = 1 - binom_h0[:15 + diff - 1].sum()
    left_sq  = binom_h0[:15 - diff].sum()
    return left_sq + right_sq

pvalue_two_sided_sum(30, 21)
```

### Asymmetric distribution (any $\mu_0$)

```{python}
def two_sided_test_nonsym(n, mu, alpha):
    c2 = int(binom.ppf(1 - alpha / 2, n, mu)) + 1
    c1 = int(binom.ppf(    alpha / 2, n, mu)) - 1
    return c1, c2

two_sided_test_nonsym(30, 0.8, 0.05)
```

```{python}
def pvalue_two_sided(n, q, mu=0.5):
    pvalue_left  = binom.cdf(q, n, mu)
    pvalue_right = 1 - binom.cdf(q - 1, n, mu)
    return 2 * min(pvalue_left, pvalue_right)

pvalue_two_sided(30, 28, mu=0.8)
```

---

## Power of a statistical test

$$\text{Power} = 1 - \beta = P(Q \in S \mid H_1)$$

```{python}
def get_stat_power(N, mu_h0, mu_alternative, alpha):
    critical_value = int(binom.ppf(1 - alpha, N, mu_h0)) + 1
    return 1 - binom.cdf(critical_value - 1, N, mu_alternative)
```

```{python}
# N=30: low power
get_stat_power(N=30, mu_h0=0.5, mu_alternative=0.6, alpha=0.05)
```

```{python}
# N=300: high power
get_stat_power(N=300, mu_h0=0.5, mu_alternative=0.6, alpha=0.05)
```

Power vs. sample size (80 % threshold):

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-align: center

n_grid = np.arange(10, 601, 10)
power  = np.array([get_stat_power(N, mu_h0=0.5, mu_alternative=0.6, alpha=0.05) for N in n_grid])
min_n  = n_grid[power >= 0.8].min()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.plot(n_grid, power)
ax.axhline(0.8,   linestyle="--", color="red")
ax.axvline(min_n, linestyle="--", color=turquoise)
ax.text(min_n + 5, 0.1, f"N = {min_n}", color=turquoise, fontsize=13)
ax.set_xlabel("Sample size N", fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel("Power", fontsize=13)
ax.tick_params(labelsize=13)
plt.show()
```

---

## Minimum detectable effect (MDE)

```{python}
def binom_test_mde_one_sided(N, mu0, alpha=0.05, min_power=0.8):
    delta_grid = np.linspace(0, 1 - mu0, 500)
    power      = np.array([get_stat_power(N, mu0, mu0 + d, alpha) for d in delta_grid])
    fit_delta  = delta_grid[power >= min_power]
    return fit_delta[0] if len(fit_delta) > 0 else None

binom_test_mde_one_sided(N=30, mu0=0.5, alpha=0.05, min_power=0.8)
```

---

## Confidence intervals

A **confidence interval** is the set of $\mu_0$ values for which $H_0: \mu = \mu_0$ is *not* rejected at level $\alpha$.

### Two-sided CI (inversion method)

```{python}
success_cnt = 19
mu_grid     = np.arange(0, 1 + 1e-9, 0.001)
mu_no_rej   = []

for mu_h0 in mu_grid:
    c1, c2 = two_sided_test_nonsym(30, mu_h0, alpha=0.05)
    if success_cnt > c1 and success_cnt < c2:
        mu_no_rej.append(mu_h0)

print(f"95% CI: {min(mu_no_rej):.3f} -- {max(mu_no_rej):.3f}")
```

```{python}
success_cnt = 28
mu_no_rej   = []

for mu_h0 in mu_grid:
    c1, c2 = two_sided_test_nonsym(30, mu_h0, alpha=0.05)
    if success_cnt > c1 and success_cnt < c2:
        mu_no_rej.append(mu_h0)

print(f"95% CI: {min(mu_no_rej):.3f} -- {max(mu_no_rej):.3f}")
```

### One-sided CI

One-sided 95% CI ≡ left bound of two-sided 90% CI:

```{python}
success_cnt = 22
mu_no_rej   = []

for mu_h0 in mu_grid:
    c1, c2 = two_sided_test_nonsym(n=30, mu=mu_h0, alpha=0.1)
    if success_cnt > c1 and success_cnt < c2:
        mu_no_rej.append(mu_h0)

print(f"Two-sided 90% CI: {min(mu_no_rej):.3f} -- {max(mu_no_rej):.3f}")
```

### Wilson CI (`statsmodels`)

```{python}
np.random.seed(111)

N_EXPERIMENTS = 1000
SAMPLE_SIZE   = 30
latent_mu     = 0.5

fail = 0
for _ in range(N_EXPERIMENTS):
    q        = binom.rvs(SAMPLE_SIZE, latent_mu)
    lo, hi   = proportion_confint(q, SAMPLE_SIZE, alpha=0.05, method="wilson")
    if not (lo < latent_mu < hi):
        fail += 1

print(f"Empirical coverage: {1 - fail / N_EXPERIMENTS:.3f}")
```
